items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking standards to boost nutritious options
items.summary: USDA finalized new SNAP retailer stocking standards requiring SNAP-authorized stores to carry 7 varieties of items in each of 4 staple food categories (protein, grains, dairy, and fruits/vegetables). The agency said the changes take effect in Fall 2026 and are intended to increase access to healthier foods while closing loopholes that allowed some snack foods to count toward staple requirements.

items.title: SSA: 2026 Social Security COLA set at 2.8%, raising SSI federal payments
items.summary: Social Security’s 2026 cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) is 2.8% based on the CPI-W, according to SSA. SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists the SSI federal payment standard increasing to $994/month for an individual (up from $967) and $1,491/month for a couple (up from $1,450).

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter to help states implement a statutory change limiting the ability to claim federal Medicaid and CHIP matching funds for individuals in certain noncitizen categories. CMS said the statutory change begins October 1, 2026, while noting that federal matching funds remain available for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to lawfully residing children and pregnant women options.

items.title: IRS releases 2026 tax inflation adjustments, including higher EITC maximum for larger families
items.summary: The IRS announced annual inflation adjustments for tax year 2026, including a higher maximum Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) for taxpayers with three or more qualifying children. The IRS release lists the 2026 EITC maximum for 3+ qualifying children as $8,231 (up from $8,046), and standard deduction amounts rising to $16,100 (single), $32,200 (married filing jointly), and $24,150 (head of household).

items.title: Federal Register posts key FAFSA deadlines for 2026–27 aid year (submit by June 30, 2027)
items.summary: A Federal Register notice lists the deadline for the Department’s FAFSA Processing System to receive a 2026–2027 FAFSA as June 30, 2027. The notice also sets September 11, 2027 as the deadline for corrections (and other changes like address or school updates) and says schools must receive an ISIR or FAFSA Submission Summary by the student’s last date of enrollment for 2026–2027 or September 18, 2027—whichever is earlier.

items.title: HUD tells PHAs to run EIV Deceased Tenants Report at least monthly to reduce Section 8 payment errors
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-11 updates guidance requiring public housing agencies (PHAs) to use the EIV system’s Deceased Tenants Report to reduce subsidy payment and administrative errors across Public Housing, Housing Choice Vouchers (HCV), PBV, and Mod Rehab. HUD directs PHAs to generate the report at least once a month (and for HCV/PBV/Mod Rehab, at least monthly before disbursing Housing Assistance Payments), and the notice includes timelines like submitting Form HUD-50058 no later than 60 calendar days from the effective date of action.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: California says it received $236 million in LIHEAP funding for federal fiscal year 2026
items.summary: California’s LIHEAP program page says the state received $236 million in annual LIHEAP funding for Federal Fiscal Year 2026. The page also notes that LIHEAP funding is limited and local service providers prioritize particularly vulnerable households; it describes crisis help for households facing a 24–48 hour disconnect notice.
items.source: California Department of Community Services & Development (CSD)

items.title: USDA WIC memo: temporary higher monthly milk amounts effective immediately through Sept. 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA issued WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-1 implementing a temporary increase to the maximum monthly allowance (MMA) of fluid milk for certain WIC food packages. The memo lists updated milk MMAs (e.g., 16 quarts for Food Packages IV A/B and VI, 22 quarts for Food Packages V A/B, and 24 quarts for Food Package VII) and says the authority for updated amounts terminates September 30, 2026.
